title: Real Madrid say they can beat Bayern Munich after poor Champions League first leg. Are they right?
summary: Real Madrid faces skepticism about their chances of mounting a Champions League comeback against Bayern Munich despite their poor first-leg performance at the Bernabéu. However, their history of remarkable European comebacks suggests such a turnaround remains possible.
content: Lacking Bernabéu magic, Real Madrid can tee up a more spectacular comeback in Munich in the Champions League. They've done it before.

zh.title: 皇马表示尽管欧冠首回合表现不佳仍能击败拜仁慕尼黑，他们说对了吗？
zh.summary: 尽管皇家马德里在伯纳乌球场首回合表现不佳，但他们在欧冠对阵拜仁慕尼黑的逆转前景面临质疑。然而，他们令人瞩目的欧洲逆转历史表明这样的翻盘仍然可能。

title: World's best keeper? Raya helps Arsenal get back on track
summary: David Raya delivered a commanding performance as Arsenal's defense proved decisive in their 1-0 victory over Sporting in the Champions League quarterfinal. Raya's Arsenal teammates regard him as the world's best goalkeeper, underscoring his importance to the club's success.
content: David Raya is the best goalkeeper in the world, according to his Arsenal team-mates - and the Spaniard played a key role in his side's 1-0 win at Sporting.

zh.title: 世界最佳门神？拉亚帮助阿森纳重振雄风

title: Alexander-Arnold fails to ease Tuchel concerns as Kane stars
summary: Thomas Tuchel's concerns about Trent Alexander-Arnold's defensive capabilities persist despite the full-back's efforts, while Harry Kane continues to be England's standout performer. The assessment emerged during Tuchel's evaluation mission in Madrid.
content: Thomas Tuchel's doubts about Trent Alexander-Arnold and reliance on Harry Kane are confirmed on the England manager's Madrid mission.

zh.title: 阿诺德未能消解图赫尔担忧 凯恩表现出众

title: Sacked in the Morning

title: Arsenal 'had a point to prove' after 'difficult period' - Arteta
summary: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta credited his side's determination to bounce back after a difficult period with consecutive defeats as they secured a first-leg advantage against Sporting in the Champions League quarterfinal. Kai Havertz's stoppage-time winner exemplified the team's response.
content: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta says his side "had a point to prove" following back-to-back defeats and praises his side's reaction, as Kai Havertz's stoppage-time winner secures a first-leg advantage in their Champions League quarter-final against Sporting.

title: Football Daily

The gossip column highlights several transfer movements among Premier League clubs.

title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies fives days after resigning as Romania coach

title: 'We've become quite experienced in negativity'
